pi_items.source: CAS Registry Number verified via multiple chemical databases including AccuStandard and NIST sources

NIST literature and research papers show Tc = 512.75 ± 0.1 K; also 513.15 K from WebBook. Task value of 512.6 K is within experimental uncertainty but slightly lower than standard NIST reference of 512.75-513.15 K
NIST critical property data and Freon technical documentation confirm 80.972 bar for methanol
CAS Registry Number verified as standard identifier for toluene via NIST and multiple chemical databases
NIST Chemistry WebBook toluene entry shows 92.1384 amu
NIST data confirms Tc = 591.75 K (also reported as 591.749 K). Multiple sources including NIST measurements and Chegg chemistry references confirm this exact value
NIST thermophysical data shows Pc = 4.1263 MPa = 41.263 bar. Converted from standard NIST pressure units to bar as required
CAS Registry Number verified via AccuStandard and NIST sources for 1,1,1,2-Tetrafluoroethane (R-134a/Freon 134a)
NIST Chemistry WebBook confirms molecular weight of 102.0309 amu for R-134a
NIST ITS-90 standard critical temperature for R-134a is Tc = 374.179 K (also confirmed in Modified Benedict-Webb-Rubin equation of state formulations)
NIST critical properties database shows Pc = 4.056 MPa = 40.56 bar for R-134a. Task value of 40.59 bar matches within rounding error (0.03 bar difference, 0.07%)
NIST Chemistry WebBook is the official U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology database at https://webbook.nist.gov/chemistry/

Specified in task metadata and used in verifier.py for numerical tolerance checks on MW, Tc, and Pc values

privileged_info_summary: The task requires research of three industrial fluids' thermophysical properties from NIST Chemistry WebBook. All reference data values have been independently verified against NIST sources: Methanol (CAS 67-56-1) with MW=32.04 g/mol, Tc=512.6 K (NIST: 512.75±0.1 K), Pc=80.97 bar (verified); Toluene (CAS 108-88-3) with MW=92.14 g/mol, Tc=591.75 K (exact NIST match), Pc=41.26 bar (NIST: 41.263 bar); R-134a (CAS 811-97-2) with MW=102.03 g/mol, Tc=374.21 K (NIST: 374.179 K), Pc=40.59 bar (NIST: 40.56 bar). All critical pressures must be reported in bar units per task requirements. The data is real (from NIST), not synthetic. The 2.0% tolerance in the verifier accommodates minor experimental variations across different NIST determinations.
